prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for furan (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. No density fitting (exact four-centre integrals). Partial charges via Mulliken scheme. N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz, single-point (no re-optimization). All three SCFs converged. Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true. Charge conservation: sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. No warnings reported. Headline value: most electrophilic atom is C (atom 5, 1-based), f+ = 1.9242931. Most nucleophilic is also atom 5 (C), f- = 0.1616371. The alpha-carbons C4/C5 are the dominant reactive sites by both f+ and the dual descriptor (~+1.76). Mulliken f+ values go negative at O and H atoms — a known artefact of the Mulliken population scheme (not constrained to [0,1]); relative carbon rankings are physically meaningful. No warnings were issued by the engine.
